(ナカヤマフェスタ; foaled April 5, 2006)[[1]](#cite_note-foal_date-1) is a champion Japanese [thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") racehorse, which is most notable for winning the 2010 [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen") and running second to [Workforce](/wiki/Workforce_(horse) "Workforce (horse)") in the 2010 [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe").

**Nakayama Festa** (ナカヤマフェスタ; foaled April 5, 2006)[[1]](#cite_note-foal_date-1) is a champion Japanese [thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") racehorse, which is most notable for winning the 2010 [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen") and running second to [Workforce](/wiki/Workforce_(horse) "Workforce (horse)") in the 2010 [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe").

## Pedigree

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nakayama_Festa&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Pedigree")]

Nakayama Festa was sired by the 2001 [Hong Kong Vase](/wiki/Hong_Kong_Vase "Hong Kong Vase") winner [Stay Gold](/wiki/Stay_Gold_(horse) "Stay Gold (horse)"), who was in turn sired by the influential [American](/wiki/United_States "United States")-bred sire [Sunday Silence](/wiki/Sunday_Silence "Sunday Silence"). Nakayama Festa's dam, Dear Wink, was sired by the American stallion Tight Spot, which was a Grade 1 stakes winner in the United States and has since been exported to [China](/wiki/China "China") for stud services.[[2]](#cite_note-pedigree-2)

## Racing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nakayama_Festa&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Racing career")]

Nakayama Festa won the Nisai Stakes as a two-year-old, which was his only placing in 2008. He only won one race (St Lite Kinen) as a 3-year old and placed in the 2009 [Keisei Hai](/wiki/Keisei_Hai "Keisei Hai"). Nakayama Festa won the 2010 [Takarazuka Kinen](/wiki/Takarazuka_Kinen "Takarazuka Kinen") in Japan by beating two-time champion [filly](/wiki/Filly "Filly") [Buena Vista](/wiki/Buena_Vista_(horse) "Buena Vista (horse)").[[3]](#cite_note-Takarazuka_Kinen-3) He also finished second at the 2010 [Prix Foy](/wiki/Prix_Foy "Prix Foy") and [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe") in France.[[1]](#cite_note-foal_date-1) Nakayama Festa ran in two races in 2011. He finished in last place out of a field of four horses at the [Prix Foy](/wiki/Prix_Foy "Prix Foy") run at [Longchamp Racecourse](/wiki/Longchamp_Racecourse "Longchamp Racecourse") in France, the race ultimately being won by the French horse [Sarafina](/wiki/Sarafina_(horse) "Sarafina (horse)").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the final start of his career on 2 October 2011 at the same course, Nakayama Festa finished in 11th place out of a field of 16 horses in the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe, which was won by [Danedream](/wiki/Danedream "Danedream").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nakayama_Festa&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Racing form")]

***Legend:***
  
  Turf

## Stud career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nakayama_Festa&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Stud career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/46/%E3%83%8A%E3%82%AB%E3%83%A4%E3%83%9E%E3%83%95%E3%82%A7%E3%82%B9%E3%82%BF_%E3%81%86%E3%82%89%E3%81%8B%E3%82%8F%E5%84%AA%E9%A7%BF%E3%83%93%E3%83%AC%E3%83%83%E3%82%B8%E3%82%A2%E3%82%A8%E3%83%AB%E3%81%AB%E3%81%A6%E3%80%82%282024.02%29.jpg/250px-%E3%83%8A%E3%82%AB%E3%83%A4%E3%83%9E%E3%83%95%E3%82%A7%E3%82%B9%E3%82%BF_%E3%81%86%E3%82%89%E3%81%8B%E3%82%8F%E5%84%AA%E9%A7%BF%E3%83%93%E3%83%AC%E3%83%83%E3%82%B8%E3%82%A2%E3%82%A8%E3%83%AB%E3%81%AB%E3%81%A6%E3%80%82%282024.02%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:%E3%83%8A%E3%82%AB%E3%83%A4%E3%83%9E%E3%83%95%E3%82%A7%E3%82%B9%E3%82%BF_%E3%81%86%E3%82%89%E3%81%8B%E3%82%8F%E5%84%AA%E9%A7%BF%E3%83%93%E3%83%AC%E3%83%83%E3%82%B8%E3%82%A2%E3%82%A8%E3%83%AB%E3%81%AB%E3%81%A6%E3%80%82(2024.02).jpg)

Nakayama Festa in February 2024

Nakayama Festa was retired from racing in October 2011 to [stand](/wiki/Stud_farm "Stud farm") at the Breeders Stallion Station in [Hidaka, Hokkaido](/wiki/Hidaka,_Hokkaido "Hidaka, Hokkaido") for the 2012 breeding season. On the decision to retire Nakayama Festa, his trainer Yoshitaka Ninomiya remarked, "He doesn't have any injuries but we thought it was better for him to retire when his condition was good."[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Nakayama Festa retired from stud duty in 2023 and was pensioned at the Urakawa Yushun Village AERU in [Urakawa, Hokkaido](/wiki/Urakawa,_Hokkaido "Urakawa, Hokkaido").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nakayama_Festa&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

An [anthropomorphized](/wiki/Moe_anthropomorphism "Moe anthropomorphism") version of Nakayama Festa appears in *[Umamusume: Pretty Derby](/wiki/Umamusume:_Pretty_Derby "Umamusume: Pretty Derby")*, voiced by [Shino Shimoji](/wiki/Shino_Shimoji "Shino Shimoji").[[10]](#cite_note-10)
